source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A uniform \( 165\,\text{N} \) bar is supported horizontally by two identical wires. A small \( 185\,\text{N} \) cube of lead is placed three-fourths of the way from \( A \) to \( B \). The wires are each \( 75.0\,\text{cm} \) long and have a mass of \( 5.50\,\text{g} \). If both of them are simultaneously plucked at the center, what is the frequency of the beats that they will produce when vibrating in their fundamental?

normalization_record.normalized_question_text: A uniform \( 165\,\text{N} \) bar is supported horizontally by two identical wires. A small \( 185\,\text{N} \) cube of lead is placed three-fourths of the way from \( A \) to \( B \). The wires are each \( 75.0\,\text{cm} \) long and have a mass of \( 5.50\,\text{g} \). If both of them are simultaneously plucked at the center, what is the frequency of the beats that they will produce when vibrating in their fundamental?
